events.title: Celebrate SWANA Month! Storytime and Craft
events.description: Join Librarian Martha for SWANA Storytime, a welcoming storytime celebrating the cultures of Southwest Asia and North Africa. Families will enjoy picture books, songs, and a simple craft inspired by SWANA traditions—featuring stories from countries such as Iran, Afghanistan, Armenia, Lebanon, Syria, Egypt, Morocco, and more. Designed for young children and their caregivers, this program supports early literacy while uplifting community voices and joyfully sharing diverse cultural heritage.

events.title: ONLINE: Health Talk - Hype or Health?
events.description: In our April Health Talk, you will learn about “wellness fads,” and what actually supports your health and wellbeing.Presenter: Sheila Chang, MD, ABLM – Kaiser Permanente board-certified Internal Medicine and Lifestyle Medicine PhysicianAfter this session, you will be able to:Distinguish health "hypes" from evidence-based practices.Explore different wellness trends and identify which ones are helpful (or not) – wearables, recovery tools, supplements, diet trends.Understand key lifestyle habits that drive the greatest health and performance benefits.Apply a practical, sustainable approach to wellness that fits your everyday life.Register in advance for this webinar.Once you register, you will receive a confirmation email with a link to the Microsoft Teams meeting link.If you have questions about the program, please contact: librarians@santaclaraca.govFor recording of past programs, please visit this link:https://mydoctor.kaiserpermanente.org/ncal/article/kp-health-talks-sca-health-educa

events.title: Adult Book Discussion: They Called Us Enemy (Online)
events.description: Come discuss George Takei'sThey Called Us Enemy. In this graphic memoir, Takei describes his childhood experience of being incarcerated with his family in a prison camp during World War II.They Called Us Enemyis aNew York Timesbestseller and winner of numerous awards.Read the book in advance of the discussion. Then come and share what you noticed, what you felt and, if relevant, your relationship to the history the memoir describes. Copies of the book will be available at the Information Desk for discussion registrants while supplies last. Registration does not guarantee a copy of the book.This discussion is for adults and will take place online over Zoom. Registration is required.Alternately, anin-person book discussionwill take place Sunday, May 31 at 4 p.m. It follows a livestreamedauthor talk with George Takeiat 2 p.m. the same day.Register for Adult Book Discussion: They Called Us Enemy (Online)This program is part of One Book, One Coast, a multi-state book club. events.title: The Librarians Movie Screening and Discussion
events.location: City Hall, 456 W. Olive Avenue, Sunnyvale, California, 94086
events.description: Join us for a screening and discussion of the award-winning documentaryThe Librarians. The movie will be screened at Sunnyvale City Hall Council Chambers at 456 West Olive Ave.In Texas, the Krause List targets 850 books focused on race and LGBTQ+ stories—triggering sweeping book bans across the U.S. at an unprecedented rate. As tensions escalate, librarians connect the dots from heated school and library board meetings nationwide to lay bare the underpinnings of extremism fueling the censorship efforts. Despite facing harassment, threats, and laws aimed at criminalizing their work—the librarians’ rallying cry for freedom to read is a chilling cautionary tale.Registration is not required.AccessibilityPursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act, the City of Sunnyvale will make reasonable efforts to accommodate persons with disabilities. If you require special accommodation, please contact the library at 408-730-7300 orchat with a librarianat least three days in advance of the program

events.description: Join the San José Youth Empowerment Alliance for a Peace Walk in the Hoffman Via Monte neighborhood, celebrating community strength and unity.The Peace Walk will start at 10 a.m. at Payne Avenue Friendship Park and end at 11 a.m. at Rosemary Elementary School.Youth Violence Prevention Week 2026Youth Violence Prevention Week 2026 is celebrated from Saturday, April 18, 2026 through Saturday, April 25, 2026. This week is a coordinated, citywide initiative designed to elevate youth voice, strengthen cross-sector partnerships, and advance community-based violence prevention strategies.Through a collective impact model, the City of San José, the San José Youth Empowerment Alliance, local schools, and community partners are delivering eight days of school-based youth activations and community engagement events.

events.title: Virtual Author Talk - Harrison Browne (Online)
events.description: Let Us Play: Winning the Battle for Gender Diverse Athletessubverts misconceptions around the participation of gender diverse athletes—advocating for the inclusion of trans and nonbinary athletes across all levels of sport.The debate over the inclusion of gender diverse people in sport has become the latest battleground in the fight for basic human rights and equality. InLet Us Play, trans athlete Harrison Browne and investigative journalist Rachel Browne reveal how the opposition towards gender diverse athletes is fueled by fear and a moral panic as opposed to facts around what makes “a level playing field.”Join us for an online talk with Harrison Browne. In addition to co-authoringLet Us Play, Browne is a former professional hockey player, activist, filmmaker and actor. He directed and starred in the moviePink Lightand was recently in the TV seriesHeated Rivalry.This author talk will take place online. Registration is required.Register for Virtual Author Talk - Harrison BrowneAccessi

events.title: Herstory 1: The Legal History of Chinese American Women
events.description:
Join SJPL’s Berryessa Branch for  Herstory 1 , a compelling exhibition exploring the legal struggles and achievements of Chinese American women over the past 150 years. 
 In the 1870s, some of the first Chinese women arriving in the United States were unjustly labeled “lewd” by immigration officials because of their traditional dress. They challenged these accusations in court — and won — helping to establish early legal protections. 
 Through historic photographs and powerful stories,  Herstory

If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.description: Are you new to the Santa Clara City Library? Or maybe you just want to learn more about all the great FREE services you can access with your library card? Attend our virtual program to learn how to maximize your library card, including how to navigate our library website and catalog, how to access databases and electronic resources, how to learn about free library events, and more! You'll also hear about some of our library staff's favorite services at the library. We don't want the "best kept secrets" of the library to be secret - we want to share the good news with you!Register in advance for this webinar on Zoom.A recording will be available on theLibrary's YouTube pageafter the event.

events.title: Reading Buddies
events.description: Reading Buddies provides students in grades K-3 with an opportunity to improve their reading skills by working one-on-one with a volunteer. Reading Buddies creates a positive reading experience for beginning and developing readers and helps children build confidence as readers. Our volunteers will listen to children read and provide support in phonics, sight words, and comprehension. Kids will have a 10-15-minute session with a dog to read out loud the book of their choice (if you don't have a book, a librarian will suggest one). The main purpose is to provide a relaxed, friendly, and non-judgmental atmosphere, so a child can read aloud to practice their reading skills.  
 Reading out loud may enhance comprehension

events.description:
Learn how to support your friends, families, and neighbors by becoming certified in   Mental Health First Aid  . During these monthly tabling visits, the TRUST team provides information about their services and resources to our community members.Central Park Library- Every first Thursday of the month, 3-5 p.m.Mission Branch Library- Every second Wednesday of the month, 10 a.m.- 12 p.m.Northside Branch Library- Every third Tuesday of the month, 1-3 p.m.If you have any questions about this library service, please emaillibrarians@santaclaraca.govor call 408-615-2900TRUST is a community service that helps Santa Clara County residents during urgent mental health or substance use situations. TRUST treats every situation on a case-by-case basis, providing responsive care without involving law enforcement and triaging other teams when needed.TRUST call center and field response teams are available 24/7 to answer calls for help. events.title: *Virtual* Time Management with Adults
events.description: *This is a FREE webinar*     About this event:  Juggling work, family, and personal goals can feel overwhelming. events.title: COMMUNITY MEETING NOTICE: NEW THOMPSON MINI PARK, PROJECT 26-35
events.location: Multi-Use Room, Monta Loma Elementary School, 460 Thompson Avenue, Mountain View, CA
events.description: A new 0.43-acre mini park is proposed at the properties located at 538 Thompson Avenue and 2231 W. Middlefield Road to provide additional park and open space in the Monta Loma neighborhood. The project will create a welcoming and functional space for everyday park use.This first community meeting will provide an overview of the project and invite input on desired park amenities. After this meeting, staff will return to the community with concept plans for input at a future date.Please join us at the meeting to share your thoughts on this exciting park project.If you have any questions about this project, contact Tsan Liu, Senior Project Manager at (650) 903-6533 or e-mailtsan.liu@mountainview.gov.

events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.title: Deer Hollow Storytime
events.description:

Meet us there: Deer Hollow Farm, 22500 Cristo Rey Dr., Cupertino

Please note: the parking lot for Deer Hollow Farm is about a one-mile flat, paved walk to the farm. We recommend walking shoes, hats, and/or strollers. Also, there will be limited seating-- prepare to sit on the floor if you can.

events.title: Itty Bitty Jamboree
events.description:

If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

events.title: Friday Fun : Gaman Art Workshop
events.description:
Join us for a hands-on workshop exploring the history of Gaman Art—art created by Japanese Americans during World War II incarceration as a way to express resilience, creativity, and hope. 
 Participants will design and create their own bird pins using shrink plastic, transforming simple materials into meaningful works of art. This program invites participants to reflect on identity, storytelling, and the power of creativity in times of hardship.  This event is free and all materials are provide

All experience levels welcome. Recommended for grades 2-6.

events.title: Meet the Herptiles: Reptiles, Amphibians and More!
events.location: Hidden Villa — 26870 Moody Road, Los Altos Hills
events.description: Join us to find out which snakes and lizards live in our area, which ones are dangerous, and which of them are hugely helpful on an organic farm like Hidden Villa's. See slides and preserved specimens up close, and learn why the usually sluggish California Slender Salamander might just be one of the fastest land animals around!

events.title: Beam Me Up, Sulu: Rediscovering a Lost Story Film Screening
events.description:

Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

Our ESL (English as a Second Language) / Learn English conversation group program is a friendly and informal environment for adults (18 years old and older) to practice speaking and listening in English. events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.description: Do you have things that you like, but no longer work? Don’t toss them, repair them! events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.title: STEM Wednesday: Experimenting With Our Eyes
events.description: Science From Scientists presents: Experimenting With Our Eyes!  This program will walk students through the structures of their eyes, from back to front. Students explore the distribution and behavior of the rod and cone cells of their own retinas through experiments with peripheral vision, afterimages (an experiment with color vision), and blind spots. They will have the opportunity to investigate the focal length of their eye’s lens, and the light response of their iris.  Recommended for ages

Alternatively, pick a black & white image to engrave into your keychain or add custom text. Supplies limited to 1 single-sided keychain per participant.

events.title: Gaiascope
events.description: Artist Brooke Einbender will presentGaiascopeat Circle of Palms. The installation features three suspended kaleidoscope sculptures supported by steel cables. Each six-foot-long structure includes a laser-cut steel exterior with intricate patterns that diffuse LED light into dynamic, color-changing displays. Inside, mirrored chambers showcase video art exploring natural wonders—from the micro to the macro. Interactive pedestals allow participants to alter lighting sequences, colors, video content, and playback speed.

events.description: Reading Buddies provides students in grades K-3 with an opportunity to improve their reading skills by working one-on-one with a reading coach. Reading Buddies creates a positive reading experience for beginning and developing readers and helps children build confidence as readers. Our reading coach will listen to children read and provide support in reading and comprehension. Students can bring their favorite book or choose from the available selection at the Berryessa Branch Library.   Fr

events.title: Family Storytime
events.description:
Storytime is designed to accommodate a mix of ages and invites the entire family to participate in early literacy activities such as stories, songs, rhymes, fingerplays, and movement activities. Storytime is a fun and interactive experience for the whole family that promotes the joys of reading and learning for all ages. This program is geared for children of all ages and their parents/caregivers! 
 This program is developed to align with  the Early Education Quality Standards .  
  Free. No reg

events.title: Art Club
events.description:
Visit the library to participate in arts and crafts for kids.  All tools and supplies are provided. 
 Free. No registration required.

events.title: Board Game Day
events.description:
Join us for a volunteer-led hour of family friendly board games on the third Sunday of every month. The library will have games on hand, but participants are also welcome to bring their own games to play at the library. Please bring games that are appropriate for all ages. Light refreshments will be served.   Free. No registration required.      
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: CENTRAL: Foundation and Friends Patio Book Sale
Family Games: Cards & Board Games
events.description: Children, teens, and their families are invited to play a selection of board games and card games. The games included will be a mix of strategy-based games, educational games, and luck-based games. Come together to have fun with friends and family!   ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Lego® Club!
events.description:
Come join us every Sunday for Lego® Club!  We have tons of Lego® bricks for you to build and explore!  Duplo® bricks are also available for our younger friends.  
  Want to push yourself and try something a little more difficult?  Ask the librarian for a Lego® Challenge!   Important:  Children under 5 must be accompanied by their parents as small LEGO ® pieces can be choking hazards.     ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Make Music Day: Sharat Lin
events.description:
For Make Music Day, Bascom Library will feature the dancer Sharat Lin! 
 Sharat Lin performs the Dance of Peace that interprets passionate positive music and lyrics into inspiring movements that instill peace and joy into every heart and engage people in building inclusive community.   He invites audiences to join in dancing, movement, or simply making rhythms.  Recommended for children and families. 
  Registration recommended . Walk-ins will be allowed in as space permits.   ADA Accommoda

events.title: Board Game Zone
events.description:
Join us every Sunday between 2:00 - 4:00 PM at the Seven Trees Library to play fun board games! We have a large selection to choose from, including new games and classic games that everyone of all ages can enjoy. 
 No Registration Required. 
  Thanks to the Friends of the Seven Trees Library for making this possible!  
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Leyendo en español para niños - Reading to Children in Spanish
events.description:
Nuestra voluntaria maravillosa, leerá cuentos en español para niños y sus familias.  La participación en este programa es una manera excelente para que los niños desarrollen su imaginación y despierten el amor por los libros.

events.title: Queer Readers' Cafe - Pride Edition
events.description:
Queer joy shall abound at the Queer Readers’ Book Café!  
 This time around we'll be celebrating Pride month! 
 The Queer Readers' Cafe is sort of like a silent book club, but chatter is OK. Unlike a typical book club, participants can read whatever they want!  
 We’ll create an LGBTQ+ friendly space in our community room for folks to gather, read, and discuss what they’re reading. A selection of queer reading materials will be available to browse and checkout. Light snacks and refreshments

events.title: Reading to Children
events.description:
Participating in the Reading to Children program is a great way for children to develop their imagination and spark a love for books, reading and learning.  
 Children will enjoy one-on-one or small group interaction with our adult volunteer. They are also welcome to practice reading to our volunteer. Pre-registration is not required, and children may drop in to hear a story any time during the program. events.description:
Come join us at Cambrian Branch Library for our LEGO® Club! We'll supply both big and small LEGO® pieces, so come and bring your creativity!  
 Important:  Children under 5 must be accompanied by their parents as small LEGO ® pieces can be choking hazards. 
 No registration required.

events.description:
Interested in learning how to program a robot? If so, the Robotics with Java Workshop is perfect for you. Learn basic Java syntax and gain experience with programming a robot.  
  Note:  Participants are highly recommended to bring laptops.  
 Walk-ins are welcome. No registration required. 
 Recommended for children in grades 5 to 8.

events.title: One-On-One Tech Help
events.description:
Do you need help using a computer? Want to set up an email account? Stop by the Santa Teresa Branch Library for a session with our experienced volunteer! They can assist you with basic tech issues, such as:  
  Setting up an email account, composing & sending emails, using email attachments   Web searches and basic data entry   Learning the basics of Microsoft Word and Excel and web-based productivity software such as Google docs   Using the library's pay-for-print and photocopy machines  Downlo

events.title: Chess Club for Kids
events.description: Come learn to play chess, or just play a game! This is an informal and welcoming program for kids up to age 12 and their families.  Chess Club meets every Monday. Please check the website event listing for possible date cancellations and library holidays.   ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Garba Folk and Bollywood Dancing
events.description:
Those going to 3rd to 5th grade next fall are welcome to come and learn Garba folk and Bollywood style dancing. No experience required. Space is limited. Free.    Registration required for the entire series. Register only once for the entire series.  Must arrive on time for the first class to avoid losing your spot for the entire series. A recital for students will be held on Monday, July 27 at 4:00pm. 
  ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Make It Mondays
events.description: Join us every Monday for arts and crafts at the library! Each week will feature a different activity that focuses on building creativity, social-emotional, and school readiness skills through art. All supplies will be provided!    ADA Accommodation Requests

events.title: Santa Teresa Junior Math Club
events.description:
Want to grow in understanding basic math concepts and working on basic competitive math skills? Join us in Santa Teresa Junior Math Club and see your math and problem-solving skills improve! Concepts covered include rounding, solving with fractions, area, perimeter, data and graphs, money, polygons, angles, and decimals. 
 For children in 3rd through 5th grade (geared towards ages 8-11 years old). 
  Free. No registration required, but you can register online if you'd like reminder emails around
